raw_input:
[4.0/5] scent is too much for me on this one, but there other ones are awesome; great nourishing scrub This is my second scrub with this company. The first was great, but I find that the fragrance is too much for me with this Honey one. These products do contain "parfum" as an added ingredient, but it is last on the list. I have included a picture of the ingredients as I do not see the list anywhere else. My other salt scrub by this company-bloodorange, and that scent is wonderful and fresh, in bottle, in shower, and on skin after (very light). This one is just heavy, rather perfume like. I have noticed some shea containing products have that, but the other one that I do like has shea as well but isn't cloying. So I don't know. I'm afraid it might trigger a headache for me. I may have to have someone else in the household finish it off. Love the other one I mentioned, though!

As for how it works- no problem. A thick frosting like creamy texture with a hint of grit. It does a nice job cleaning, polishing, and leaving the skin feel smooth and moisturized. It is a gentle scrub- very gentle. Definitely softens skin. I scoop out a generous 1/8-1/4 cup amount to use on my body. With that amount, I expect several uses from this squat container. I particularly like this one for my legs and feet as they are the most neglected in terms of skin care.

I really do like Natural Soultion salt scrubs, and it appears that there are 4 kinds. This one based solely on the fragrance is a miss for me, but another one happened to be a hit for me, which I will continue to use as needed.

EDIT: While a whiff from the container is too strong for me, the actual aroma in the shower continues to be pretty tame. It dawned on me to smell my skin after showering (something I didn't do with prior showers prior to initial review), and my skin doesn't have that strong smell that I note from the container. It smells pleasant and light, and I wanted to come back to say so.SO I just wanted to say that I haven't had a headache, and I don't actually smell like the scent from the container- I was concerned I would have that perfume smell. It isn't there, thankfully.

I'd boost it to a 5 rating, but the product descriptions says, "No Artificial Fragrances Or Perfumes", and "parfum" is on ingredient list- and doesn't say where that is derived from. [5.0/5] Really good stuff! This body scrub is really nice. It smells so good and it smooths the and nourishes the skin perfectly. When you rub this gently onto your skin then rinse it off you can instantly feel the difference. It feels as if all the dead skin has been removed and your pores have been opened. It leaves you soft and refreshed as well as incredibly relaxed.
This is a big jar as well that you get and it's worth it for the price. It's definitely worth giving a try if you are interested and able to. It's really good. I love it. [3.0/5] Very small salt crystals, but smells good! The best way to describe the scent of this exfoliating scrub is to say it smells like you just walked into a day spa. It's a truly lovely smell, light, fresh, salty, and luxurious. I've actually tried using it as a lotion instead because the fragrance is so nice, but it didn't really work out. It's very creamy, though, and when you put a dollop in your hand, it might seem like there aren't any salt crystals in it, because the cream is thick like lotion.
There was quite a bit of settling in the packaging when I opened the jar, and since I ended up using quite a bit in the shower, I don't think it'll last very long--perhaps a month. The salt crystals are very small, and when I tried to vigorously scrub, it washed away before I could properly exfoliate.
How I recommend using it, in order to get the best use out of the Himalayan salt is to wet your leg before you step unto the shower, so it's a little more than damp. Then take a dollop and scrub your leg, and when you're done with whatever appendages you wanted to exfoliate, hop in the shower and rinse it off. Somehow, when my legs and arms were really drenched, the scrub just slid right off. Less moisture helped it adhere better. The smell really is great and refreshing, and once I've figured out how to use it, I've started enjoying it more. [5.0/5] Love this scrub This is the most amazing sugar scrub. I feel like I've been to the spa after a quick shower at home. Hoes on smooth Lily. Rinses cleanly. Soft relaxing scent. Not too harsh on my sensitive skin. I love it. [4.0/5] Gentle Body Scrub A nice body scrub, not too abrasive. However, I didn’t feel that it was that great as an exfoliating scrub and I found the smell to be a bit overwhelming. [4.0/5] works great I like this body scrub, it works great and smells nice too. It works great in the winter to get rid of that dry dull skin. It left my skin feeling soft and smooth. easy to use and left my skin feeling great. A lot of things in one scrub. very happy with it. [4.0/5] Hard to use in shower This product smells good and feels great, and works very well with dry hands. The only issue I have is when trying to use in the shower, most of it goes down the drain. It just falls off of my hands and skin. It is so slippery, most of it falls the the shower floor before I can get it rubbed on lol. It works very well if my skin is dry, I can actually rub it on and scrub, but if I'm wet, i can't manage it. Lol. Now using a nice thick wash cloth helps, but I just feel like my money is literally going down the drain. Hahahahahaha

In order to use this, I get in the shower, scrub with the body scrub, and THEN turn on the water to rinse, wash hair etc. This works well, and I do feel really nice afterwards. I really enjoy the smell too. [5.0/5] Naturally Wonderful I love anything with Himalayan Pink salt and this product is no exception. I like that it feels natural, like I can make it in my kitchen and It mixes easily with my shower gel. My skin feels so soft and smooth after using this. When the seasons change, I like to use an exfoliating scrub because I tend to have dry, dead skin. This scrub is exactly what I need to transition from warm weather to cooler temperatures. Definitely, recommend this product. [5.0/5] Very pleasant to use, and moisturizing! This is a really nice body scrub which is very pleasant to use. The honey base is not overly oily, and even without a heavy amount of scrubbing it does a great job of busting through more stubborn buildup of old skin, etc. I like it a lot, and will definitely be using this more! [5.0/5] Great scrub!!! This scrub smells good. It's a very warm, comforting scent. I used this scrub in the shower after I shaved to exfoliate my legs and loved how it left my legs feeling. It's also really hydrating, so I didn't feel like I needed to immediately slather on lotion... [4.0/5] Great product all around! I love this exfoliating scrub! I tried the one with honey and it smells great, works amazing and left my skin feeling like new! I gave 4 stars because the seal was not on and it leaked out, I worried that it had been tampered with but I didn't see any obvious signs that it had. I tried this on my hands first and had no adverse reactions so I used it on my face and body thereafter. This did burn a little on my face but so does any exfoliating scrub as I have somewhat sensitive skin. It made my skin so silky soft and moisturized! I thought it would be sticky as it contains honey and it wasn't sticky at all. The price is very fair considering this has no artificial ingredients and no chemicals. It didn't leave me feeling greasy or oily, just buttery smooth and clean. I'm in love with this stuff and will be buying more! [3.0/5] Yogurty Texture With a Bit of Salt My skin is soft after using this body scrub, but I don't think I can exactly attribute it solely to this scrub. Contrary to public opinion, I liked the smell. It has a think yogurty texture with just a bit of textured Himalayan salt, not sure you can call it a scrub.

I was surprised when I opened the container. When I opened the lid to the cream inside. The foil cover seamed to already be broken. Made me unsure if I should be putting it on my body. And the product seam to be organically spooned in the container, it reminded me of a homemade product. But after all that worry, my skin is fine and I am fine.

Over all I think it is fine. If you are wanting a scrub, this is not the product for you. If you are looking for a moisturizer, this may be the product for you. [4.0/5] Great product all around! I love this exfoliating scrub! I tried the one with honey and it smells great, works amazing and left my skin feeling like new! I gave 4 stars because the seal was not on and it leaked out, I worried that it had been tampered with but I didn't see any obvious signs that it had. I tried this on my hands first and had no adverse reactions so I used it on my face and body thereafter. This did burn a little on my face but so does any exfoliating scrub as I have somewhat sensitive skin. It made my skin so silky soft and moisturized! I thought it would be sticky as it contains honey and it wasn't sticky at all. The price is very fair considering this has no artificial ingredients and no chemicals. It didn't leave me feeling greasy or oily, just buttery smooth and clean. I'm in love with this stuff and will be buying more!

response:
Here is a summary of the product reviews:

Overall, most customers are happy with this body scrub, with the majority giving it 4 or 5 stars. The most commonly praised qualities are its pleasant scent, moisturizing properties, and ability to leave skin feeling soft and smooth. Several reviewers noted it works well for sensitive skin and is not too harsh or abrasive. A few customers specifically liked the honey variety and appreciated that it is made with natural ingredients and no harsh chemicals.

The main criticisms were around the scent being too strong for some users, though one reviewer noted the smell was much lighter on the skin after rinsing than it was in the jar. A few customers felt the salt crystals were too small to provide effective exfoliation, making it feel more like a moisturizer than a scrub. One practical complaint was that the product is difficult to use in the shower when skin is wet, as it tends to slide off before it can be rubbed in. A couple of reviewers also had concerns about the seal being broken upon arrival.

Several customers offered tips for best results, such as applying the scrub to slightly damp rather than fully wet skin, and turning on the shower only after scrubbing. Overall, the product is well regarded as a gentle, nourishing scrub that leaves skin feeling soft and refreshed.
